rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use const and eq declarations with valid AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool.
Ensure type consistency between assumed types and actual data types in expressions.
Only include assumptions and guarantees in AGREE annexes when concrete bounds or named constants are provided.
Avoid Boolean-to-integer conversions in range comparisons unless the data type is explicitly Boolean in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Component type contracts should not reference subcomponent ports directly unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Visible identifiers must be declared or available in the current scope for AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not use opaque or non-Boolean data in range comparisons without explicit scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner scope rules
